title: AgentReference
description: An agent-scoped pointer to a canonical object. The meaning layer. Agents do not own objects — they own their interpretation.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Must be '0.1.0' for this version of the spec.

id.description: Unique identifier for this reference. UUID v4.

canonical_object_id.description: ID of the canonical object being referenced. Must exist in the ledger.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent creating this reference. Used for attribution.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this reference was created.

context.description: Why this agent is referencing this object. Human-readable rationale. Required — references without context are not attributable.

relevance.description: Agent's estimate of how relevant this object is to the current task. 0.0 = not relevant, 1.0 = directly relevant.

trust_weight.description: Agent's trust weighting for this object's content. 0.0 = untrusted, 1.0 = fully trusted.

time_horizon.description: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. 'P7D') or datetime indicating how long this reference remains valid. Omit if indefinite.

public_key.description: Base64-encoded raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key of the signing agent. Required when 'signature' is present. Used for TOFU key registration: first submission registers the key, subsequent submissions verify against it.

signature.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 signature over the string '{id}:{canonical_object_id}:{agent_id}:{created_at}'. Required. The control plane verifies this against the registered public key before accepting the reference.
